forecast-period.text: A trough of low pressure about eastern districts will move into the eastern states early this morning. A ridge of high pressure builds in the west. A cold front will move over the south early in the week, followed by a high pressure system, which will linger in the Bight midweek.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Areas of morning fog. Isolated showers about the agricultural areas and the far north. Possible thunderstorms in the east and north. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

Areas of morning fog. Isolated showers about the far north. Isolated showers about the agricultural area becoming scattered during the afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, tending west to northwesterly about the south during the day.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Isolated showers about the agricultural area, most likely about southern coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures near to below average. South to southwesterly winds fresh about southern coasts.

text.p: Morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Isolated showers about the agricultural area, contracting to southern coasts in the evening. Maximum temperatures slightly below average. South to southwesterly winds fresh about southern coasts. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds tending easterly in the west.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: 1 to 5 mm about the west, increasing to 5 to 15 mm east of around Coober Pedy, 15 to 25 mm about central districts and the northeast. Possible falls up to 30 to 60 mm with thunderstorms.
